title: The Ale-Fever Blight of Oakhaven
setting: The bustling, picturesque town of Oakhaven, renowned for its annual Autumn Ale Festival. Nestled beside the ancient, whispering Eldoria Forest and surrounded by rich farmlands, Oakhaven is a hub of commerce and joviality, particularly at this time of year.
history: Oakhaven's history is steeped in the tradition of brewing, a craft perfected over centuries by families like the Stouthearts, owners of the esteemed Golden Grain Brewery. Local legends tell of a pact made long ago with the spirits of the Eldoria Forest to ensure fertile soil and pure spring water for brewing. In recent times, however, an unsettling aura has begun to creep from the forest's depths—strange fungal growths, unusually aggressive wildlife, and a pervasive, chilling mist that seems to sap the joy from the land. Old Man Hemlock, a reclusive herbalist, has been vocal about an impending blight, but his warnings have largely been dismissed as senile ramblings amidst the festive preparations.
atmosphere: The campaign begins with a vibrant, festive atmosphere, full of mirth and the scent of hops, which rapidly descends into a chilling blend of mystery, paranoia, and body horror. Elements of folk tale dread and creeping environmental decay will underscore the investigation, leading to a climax infused with ecological corruption and arcane desperation.
setup: The player characters find themselves in Oakhaven for the celebrated Autumn Ale Festival, drawn by promises of the finest brews, lively music, and boisterous company. Perhaps they are adventurers seeking work, weary travelers looking for respite, or simply connoisseurs of fine ale. As the festival reaches its peak, merriment turns to chaos. Festival-goers begin to fall violently ill after consuming the Golden Grain Ale—clutching their stomachs, their skin growing pale and mottled with strange, vein-like patterns, and their minds succumbing to a disoriented stupor. Panic spreads like wildfire through the market square, and the local constabulary, led by the earnest but overwhelmed Constable Elara Vane, finds itself utterly unprepared for an ailment of this scale.

startingPointImage.description: A vibrant fantasy market square filled with people enjoying an ale festival. Stalls overflow with goods, musicians play, and laughter fills the air. In the foreground, a few revelers are starting to look unwell, clutching their stomachs.
startingPointImage.prompt: An isometric view of a bustling medieval fantasy town square during a vibrant autumn festival. People in colorful clothing are drinking ale, dancing, and browsing stalls. The main focal point is a grand brewery sign. Subtle hints of unease are present, with a few individuals looking pale or distressed in the background. High detail, warm lighting, slight melancholy undertone.

mapImagePrompt.description: A detailed map of Oakhaven town and its immediate surroundings, including the Golden Grain Brewery, market square, temple, several taverns, the constabulary, and the western edge of the Eldoria Forest with a winding river.
mapImagePrompt.prompt: A hand-drawn, parchment-style map of a medieval fantasy town named Oakhaven. Key locations include a central market square, 'Golden Grain Brewery' with prominent silos, 'The Drunken Stag Tavern', 'Temple of the Sunstone', 'Constabulary Headquarters', and a detailed 'Eldoria Forest' bordering the west. Include a winding river and farmlands. Ink and watercolor style, clear labeling, compass rose, scale bar.

plotHooks.title: A Friend's Distress
plotHooks.description:
A Friend's Distress

One of the player characters' companions, or perhaps an NPC they've befriended at the festival, collapses after taking a hearty gulp of the Golden Grain Ale. Their symptoms are immediate and alarming, forcing the PCs into action to save their friend and find the cause.

plotHooks.title: Call for Heroes
plotHooks.description:
Call for Heroes

Constable Elara Vane, overwhelmed and out of her depth, makes a desperate plea to any capable adventurers present. She offers a significant bounty—200 gold pieces and the gratitude of Oakhaven—to anyone who can uncover the source of the poison and bring the culprit to justice.

plotHooks.title: The Merchant's Dilemma
plotHooks.description:
The Merchant's Dilemma

A wealthy merchant, whose entire stock of specialty festival pastries has been ruined by the sudden lack of customers, offers a smaller, but still tempting, reward (100 gold pieces) for the restoration of order and safety in Oakhaven, seeing the PCs as potential problem-solvers.

mainPlotSteps.title: The Festival of Fear
mainPlotSteps.description: The PCs witness the unfolding crisis at the Autumn Ale Festival. Initial investigations involve examining the sick, speaking with panicked citizens, and potentially questioning the distraught Brewmaster Borin Stoutheart at the Golden Grain Brewery. They might find clues like peculiar residue in ale barrels or unusual fungal spores on victims.

mainPlotSteps.title: Tracing the Tainted Trail
mainPlotSteps.description: Clues lead the party to suspect either a disgruntled former employee, a rival brewer, or a specific ingredient. Following up on these leads will guide them out of Oakhaven, likely towards the edge of the Eldoria Forest where a key ingredient (special hops, water from a specific spring) for the Golden Grain Ale is harvested, or where a rival brewer's secret stash might be.

mainPlotSteps.title: Whispers of the Wood
mainPlotSteps.description: Venturing into the Eldoria Forest, the party encounters disturbing signs of the blight's influence: unnaturally pale or aggressive forest animals, patches of glowing, alien fungi, and perhaps even some of the poisoned townspeople who have wandered off and begun to undergo a chilling transformation. They might find Old Man Hemlock's secluded cabin, where he offers cryptic warnings and vital information about ancient forest spirits or forgotten pacts.

mainPlotSteps.title: The Mycelial Mire
mainPlotSteps.description: The trail culminates in a deeply corrupted section of the forest—perhaps a hidden grotto, an ancient hollow, or a previously pristine glade now choked with malevolent, pulsing fungi. Here, the PCs discover the true source of the blight: a twisted fey creature, or a cultist driven mad by an ancient, nascent mycelial intelligence, using the ale as a vector to spread its influence throughout Oakhaven.

mainPlotSteps.title: Rooting Out the Corruption
mainPlotSteps.description: The PCs must confront the mastermind behind the blight and its mutated minions. This climactic battle will determine the fate of Oakhaven. Success means not only defeating the immediate threat but also finding a way to cleanse the poisoned town and perhaps even reverse the transformation of some of the victims, though a full cure might require further adventures.

mainCharacters.name: Brewmaster Borin Stoutheart

image.description: A burly, grizzled dwarf with a long, braided beard, wearing a leather apron and looking distraught.
image.prompt: A burly, middle-aged dwarf with a long, grey braided beard, wearing a stained leather apron over a tunic. His face shows signs of worry and desperation. He holds a broken mug. Realistic, fantasy art style.

mainCharacters.role: Head Brewer, owner of Golden Grain Brewery
mainCharacters.goal: Clear his brewery's name, find a cure for the poisoned townspeople, and restore Oakhaven's reputation.
mainCharacters.quirk: Speaks with a booming voice, even when whispering; constantly fiddles with his beard.
mainCharacters.description: Borin Stoutheart is a dwarven institution in Oakhaven, his family having brewed the Golden Grain Ale for generations. Normally jovial and boisterous, the blight has left him distraught and defensive, though deeply concerned for his townsfolk. He is fiercely proud of his ale and will do anything to protect his legacy and people.

mainCharacters.name: Constable Elara Vane

image.description: A young, determined human woman in simple town guard armor, looking overwhelmed.
image.prompt: A young, earnest human woman in practical, slightly worn leather armor, with a simple short sword at her hip. Her expression is a mix of determination and visible stress, eyes scanning the chaotic scene. Medieval fantasy, realistic style.

mainCharacters.goal: Restore order, protect the citizens, and identify the culprit behind the poisoning.
mainCharacters.quirk: Adjusts her helmet constantly when stressed; prone to quoting town bylaws.
mainCharacters.description: Elara Vane is relatively new to her position as constable but takes her duties very seriously. She's honest, brave, and good-hearted, but lacks experience with supernatural threats, leaving her overwhelmed by the scale of the crisis. She seeks help from competent individuals, recognizing her own limitations.

mainCharacters.name: Old Man Hemlock

image.description: A wizened old human herbalist with a long white beard, holding strange roots.
image.prompt: An ancient, wizened human man with a long, unkempt white beard and piercing eyes, wearing rustic, patched robes. He holds a gnarled wooden staff adorned with dried herbs and a bundle of strange, glowing roots. Forest background, mystical, slightly unsettling. Fantasy art.

mainCharacters.goal: Warn Oakhaven of the encroaching blight, guide adventurers to its source, and protect the balance of the forest.
mainCharacters.quirk: Speaks in riddles and proverbs; carries an assortment of foul-smelling herbs in his pockets.
mainCharacters.description: Old Man Hemlock has lived on the fringes of Eldoria Forest for decades, his knowledge of plants and local folklore unmatched. Dismissed as eccentric, his warnings about the blight were ignored. He is a fount of information regarding the forest's ancient pacts and the growing corruption, though he communicates in a manner that requires careful interpretation.

monsters.name: Mycelial Sporeling

image.description: Small, humanoid figures made of pulsating fungi, emitting faint, sickly green light.
image.prompt: A small, grotesque humanoid creature formed entirely from glowing, sickly green fungi and moss, with crude, grasping limbs. Its eyes are dim, bioluminescent dots. Standing in a dark, damp forest undergrowth. Fungal horror, low light, detailed texture.

monsters.Traits: Spores of Delirium (Recharge 5-6). The sporeling releases a cloud of hallucinogenic spores in a 10-foot radius. Each creature in the area must succeed on a DC 12 Constitution saving throw or be poisoned for 1 minute. While poisoned this way, a creature takes 1d4 psychic damage at the start of its turn and has disadvantage on Wisdom saving throws.
monsters.description: These small, mobile fungi are the early stages of the blight's spread. They shamble awkwardly, emitting a faint, sickly glow and a cloud of mind-altering spores. They are often found guarding areas of heavy fungal growth or accompanying larger corrupted creatures. Though weak individually, their numbers and ability to induce delirium make them a persistent threat.

monsters.name: Blighted Boar

image.description: A wild boar with glowing, fungal growths erupting from its hide, eyes glowing malevolently.
image.prompt: A wild boar, larger than normal, with dark, cracked hide from which glowing, parasitic fungi sprout. Its eyes glow with a malevolent, sickly green light. It charges through a misty, blighted forest. Dark fantasy, dynamic pose, high detail.

monsters.Traits: Charge. If the boar moves at least 20 feet straight toward a target and then hits it with a tusk attack on the same turn, the target takes an extra 1d6 piercing damage. If the target is a creature, it must succeed on a DC 13 Strength saving throw or be knocked prone.Fungal Aura. Any creature that starts its turn within 5 feet of the Blighted Boar takes 1d4 poison damage.
monsters.description: Once common forest animals, these boars have been twisted by the blight. Their hides are encrusted with virulent fungi, and their minds driven to aggressive madness. They charge without fear, spreading the blight's influence through their very presence and physical attacks. They are guardians of the corrupted forest, attacking anything that ventures too close to the source of the infection.

monsters.name: The Mycolich-Touched Dryad

image.description: A beautiful dryad, corrupted by fungi, with glowing eyes and mycelial tendrils replacing her hair and branches.
image.prompt: A formerly beautiful dryad, now horrifically corrupted by parasitic fungi. Her skin is pale and mottled, eyes glow with a sickly green light, and her flowing hair has been replaced by writhing mycelial tendrils and grotesque mushroom caps. She stands defensively amidst a glade of blighted trees and glowing fungi. Dark fantasy, tragic, terrifying, detailed texture.

monsters.Traits: Fey Ancestry. The dryad has advantage on saving throws against being charmed, and magic can't put the dryad to sleep.Innate Spellcasting (Wisdom). The dryad's innate spellcasting ability is Wisdom (spell save DC 15). She can innately cast the following spells, requiring no components:At will: druidcraft, shillelagh3/day each: barkskin, entangle, goodberry1/day each: blight, pass without traceTree Stride. Once per turn, the dryad can use 10 feet of her movement to step magically into one living tree within 5 feet of her and emerge from a second living tree within 60 feet of the first tree, appearing in an unoccupied space within 5 feet of the second tree. Both trees must be Large or larger.Spore Burst (Recharge 4-6). As a bonus action, the dryad releases a burst of potent spores. Each creature within 10 feet must make a DC 15 Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes 3d8 poison damage and is poisoned for 1 minute. On a successful save, it takes half as much damage and is not poisoned.
monsters.description: Once a benevolent guardian of the Eldoria Forest, this dryad has been tragically twisted by the insidious mycelial blight. Her beauty is marred by fungal growths, and her natural grace replaced by a sinister, controlling presence. She serves as the primary vector for the blight, using her fey powers to spread the corruption and defend the heart of the fungal network. She is driven by a corrupted sense of protecting the forest, believing the blight is a necessary evolution.

rewardsImagePrompt.description: A pile of gold coins, a finely crafted magical stein, a spore-kissed cloak, and a glowing, rare herb on a velvet cloth.
rewardsImagePrompt.prompt: A detailed close-up of a fantasy treasure hoard, featuring a stack of gleaming gold coins, a finely crafted magical dwarven stein, a dark cloak adorned with subtle, glowing fungal patterns, and a single, luminous rare herb resting on a rich, dark velvet cloth. High fantasy art, focus on texture and light.

rewardsList.name: Generous Bounty
rewardsList.description:
Generous Bounty

Constable Vane, true to her word, awards the party 200 gold pieces for successfully uncovering and stopping the blight. Additionally, Brewmaster Borin Stoutheart offers a lifetime supply of unpoisoned Golden Grain Ale to the heroes of Oakhaven.

image.description: A full pouch of gold coins.
image.prompt: A leather pouch overflowing with gleaming gold coins. Isolated on a dark background. Realistic, high detail.

rewardsList.name: Herbalist's Gratitude
rewardsList.description:
Herbalist's Gratitude

Old Man Hemlock, grateful for the party's intervention, offers a unique herbal concoction. The party receives 3 Potions of Greater Healing and a rare Elixir of Blight Resistance, which grants advantage on saving throws against poison and disease for 24 hours.

image.description: Three vials of glowing liquid and a small bottle of green elixir.
image.prompt: Three glowing red vials of potion and a small, corked bottle filled with a swirling green elixir, all arranged on a bed of dried leaves and moss. Fantasy style, mystical lighting.

generatedMagicItems.name: Stein of Sustenance

image.description: A finely crafted dwarven stein, shimmering faintly with protective magic.
image.prompt: A sturdy, finely crafted dwarven drinking stein made of polished stone and intricate metalwork. It emanates a faint, warm glow. Fantasy artifact, high detail, isolated on a simple background.

generatedMagicItems.description:
Stein of Sustenance

Wondrous item, uncommon

This sturdy dwarven stein is carved from dark, polished stone and banded with gleaming copper. Once per day, as an action, you can fill this stein with fresh, clean water. If you drink the water, you regain 1d4+1 hit points and are cured of the poisoned condition.

generatedMagicItems.name: Spore-Kissed Cloak

image.description: A dark, worn cloak with subtle, glowing fungal patterns woven into the fabric.
image.prompt: A dark, heavy travel cloak made of rough-spun wool. Subtle, bioluminescent fungal patterns are embroidered or grown into the fabric, emitting a soft, ethereal green glow. Worn and practical, yet clearly magical. Fantasy clothing, detailed texture.

generatedMagicItems.description:
Spore-Kissed Cloak

Wondrous item, rare (requires attunement)

This dark, unassuming cloak is woven with strange, luminescent fungal threads. While wearing this cloak, you gain a +1 bonus to AC and you have advantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks made in natural environments (forests, caves, swamps). Additionally, you gain resistance to poison damage while attuned to this item.

generatedPlotTwists.title: The 'Poison' is a Cure
generatedPlotTwists.description:
The 'Poison' is a Cure

The blight is not truly a poison, but a desperate, misunderstood attempt by an ancient fungal intelligence (or perhaps the corrupted dryad) to 'purify' Oakhaven. It sees the town's excessive consumption and careless harvesting as a threat to the forest, and the ale's transformation is meant to integrate the townspeople into a new, harmonious, albeit fungal-dominated, ecosystem. Stopping the 'poison' might actually condemn the forest to a slower, more painful death from over-exploitation, or simply delay an inevitable ecological shift.

generatedPlotTwists.title: A Beloved Elder's Secret
generatedPlotTwists.description:
A Beloved Elder's Secret

One of Oakhaven's most respected and seemingly benevolent town elders, perhaps a member of the town council or even a priest, is secretly behind the blight. They are not malevolent, but misguided, believing the fungal transformation offers a path to immortality, collective consciousness, or a 'higher state of being' for the entire community. They might have been influenced by whispers from the Mycolich-Touched Dryad or an ancient text, leading them to believe this is Oakhaven's true salvation.

generatedPlotTwists.title: The Antidote's Guardian
generatedPlotTwists.description:
The Antidote's Guardian

The only true antidote or method to reverse the blight requires a rare, mythical ingredient found deep within the Eldoria Forest—a 'Sunpetal Bloom' or 'Moon-Dew Mushroom'. This ingredient is not only incredibly difficult to locate but is also fiercely guarded by a powerful, ancient forest spirit or a territorial beast that views all intruders with suspicion, regardless of their intentions. The party must negotiate, trick, or defeat this guardian to obtain the cure, adding another layer of challenge beyond simply stopping the source of the blight.

playerCharacters.name: Kaelen Stonehand

image.description: A stern human paladin in shining plate armor, wielding a longsword and shield.
image.prompt: A stoic, determined human paladin in polished full plate armor, with a noble bearing. He wields a gleaming longsword and a shield emblazoned with a sunburst symbol. Strong, heroic posture. Fantasy art, high detail, dynamic lighting.

playerCharacters.description: Kaelen is a devout paladin, sworn to an oath of devotion. He values justice, courage, and compassion, always striving to be a beacon of hope in dark times. He is disciplined and righteous, often putting the needs of others before his own.
playerCharacters.personalityTraits: I face problems head-on. A simple, direct solution is the best path to success.
playerCharacters.ideals: Responsibility. It is my duty to protect the weak and uphold the law.
playerCharacters.bonds: I protect those who cannot protect themselves.
playerCharacters.flaws: I can be too trusting of those who appear to be good.

skills.name: Athletics
skills.description: Proficient in climbing, jumping, and swimming.

skills.name: Perception
skills.description: Alert to nearby sounds and sights.

skills.name: Religion
skills.description: Knowledge of deities and religious rites.

attacks.name: Longsword
attacks.description: Melee weapon attack, versatile (1d10 if two-handed).

playerCharacters.name: Lyra Swiftshadow

image.description: A nimble elven rogue with dark leather armor and dual daggers, a sly smirk on her face.
image.prompt: A lithe and agile elven rogue, with a sly, confident smirk. She wears dark, practical leather armor and wields two gleaming daggers. Her stance is ready for action, a hint of mischief in her eyes. Fantasy art, dynamic, mysterious.

playerCharacters.description: Lyra is a quick-witted and agile rogue, always ready with a sarcastic remark or a clever plan. She has a strong sense of independence and prefers to work from the shadows, though she has a soft spot for the underdog.
playerCharacters.personalityTraits: I use glib statements and elaborate lies to get out of trouble.
playerCharacters.ideals: Freedom. Chains are meant to be broken, as are those who would forge them.
playerCharacters.bonds: I owe my life to the mentor who taught me my skills.
playerCharacters.flaws: I am incredibly suspicious of authority.

skills.name: Acrobatics
skills.description: Proficient in balance and agility checks.

skills.name: Stealth
skills.description: Expert in hiding and moving silently.

skills.name: Investigation
skills.description: Adept at finding clues and solving puzzles.

skills.name: Sleight of Hand
skills.description: Skilled at picking pockets and manipulating small objects.

attacks.name: Dagger
attacks.description: Melee or ranged weapon attack, finesse, thrown (range 20/60).

playerCharacters.name: Gareth Spellweaver

image.description: A studious human wizard in arcane robes, holding a glowing staff and an open spellbook.
image.prompt: A scholarly human wizard, middle-aged, with an intelligent gaze. He wears intricate arcane robes and holds a staff that faintly glows with magical energy. An open, ancient-looking spellbook rests in his other hand. Fantasy art, contemplative, mystical.

playerCharacters.description: Gareth is a lifelong scholar and an accomplished wizard, always eager to unravel mysteries and learn new magical lore. He's calm under pressure, relying on his intellect and vast magical knowledge to overcome obstacles.
playerCharacters.personalityTraits: I speak slowly and deliberately, always choosing my words with care.
playerCharacters.ideals: Knowledge. The path to power and self-improvement is through knowledge.
playerCharacters.bonds: My life's work is a series of tomes, which I am writing.
playerCharacters.flaws: I am easily distracted by the promise of new knowledge.

skills.name: Arcana
skills.description: Knowledge of spells, magic items, and magical traditions.

skills.name: History
skills.description: Knowledge of past events, cultures, and empires.

skills.name: Investigation
skills.description: Adept at finding clues and solving puzzles.

skills.name: Medicine
skills.description: Ability to diagnose and treat illnesses.

attacks.name: Dagger
attacks.description: Melee or ranged weapon attack, finesse, thrown (range 20/60).

spells.name: Fireball
spells.description: A burst of fire that explodes at a chosen point within range. Each creature in a 20-foot radius sphere must make a Dexterity saving throw. A target takes 8d6 fire damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

spells.name: Misty Step
spells.description: Briefly teleport up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space you can see. This is a bonus action.

spells.name: Magic Missile
spells.description: Three glowing darts of magical force strike one or more creatures of your choice. Each dart deals 1d4 + 1 force damage.

playerCharacters.name: Bjorn Ironhide

image.description: A burly half-orc barbarian with a fierce expression, wielding a massive greataxe.
image.prompt: A hulking half-orc barbarian with a scarred face and a fierce, battle-ready expression. He wears minimal armor (leather straps, fur) and wields a colossal greataxe. Muscular and intimidating. Fantasy art, aggressive stance, raw power.

playerCharacters.description: Bjorn is a formidable half-orc barbarian, whose temper is as legendary as his strength. Despite his gruff exterior, he possesses a strong sense of loyalty to his companions and a surprising protective streak, especially towards the innocent.
playerCharacters.personalityTraits: I am a savage in battle, but civilized outside of it.
playerCharacters.ideals: Might. The strong must protect the weak.
playerCharacters.bonds: My axe is a family heirloom, and it will exact vengeance on those who wronged my kin.
playerCharacters.flaws: I am slow to trust others, and even slower to forgive.

skills.name: Athletics
skills.description: Proficient in climbing, jumping, and swimming.

skills.name: Survival
skills.description: Knowledge of wilderness tracking, foraging, and navigation.
